programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.

programme.desc: Upgrading Dams for a Changing Climate The hosts explore technologies and systems that help detect and reduce the impact of natural disasters.
programme.title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life

programme.desc: Designing Thread Thread for traditional shina fabric takes a year to make, along with ultrafine mohair, made with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.
programme.title: Design Museum Japan

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan

Clumsy Inspector Jacques Clouseau (Peter Sellers) chases a jewel thief (David Niven) and his nephew (Robert Wagner).
programme.title: The Pink Panther

programme.desc: In order to help bring Nazis to justice, a U.S. agent brings a traitor's daughter to Rio de Janeiro to seduce an exiled Nazi.
programme.title: Notorious

programme.desc: Batman (Adam West) and Robin (Burt Ward) save Gotham City from the Catwoman, Joker (Cesar Romero), Penguin and Riddler.
programme.title: Batman

programme.desc: A poor Chicago secretary (Diana Ross) takes the fast lane to fame as a model and a fashion designer.
programme.title: Mahogany

programme.desc: An electrician (Richard Hatch), a TV newswoman (Kay Lenz) and a mad scientist enter a dimension ruled by a warlord (John Saxon).
programme.title: Prisoners of the Lost Universe

programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes (Basil Rathbone) and Dr. Watson (Nigel Bruce) prowl foggy moors to save Sir Henry (Richard Greene) from a killer-dog family curse.
programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles

programme.desc: A paratrooper's (Dean Martin) goofy buddy (Jerry Lewis) joins him on base and makes a mockery of maneuvers.
programme.title: Jumping Jacks

programme.desc: Several would-be heirs are forced to spend a night in a haunted house in order to collect their inheritance.
programme.title: The Cat and the Canary

programme.desc: The miserly head (Brian Donlevy) of a Hollywood studio hires a goofy paperhanger (Jerry Lewis) to spy on his employees.
programme.title: The Errand Boy

programme.desc: Controversial British officer T.E. Lawrence (Peter O'Toole) learns the culture of Arabs (Alec Guinness, Anthony Quinn) and unites their tribes against the Turks.
programme.title: Lawrence of Arabia

programme.desc: A California girl called Gidget (Sandra Dee) spends summer with surf bums called Moondoggie (James Darren) and Kahuna (Cliff Robertson).
programme.title: Gidget

programme.desc: A detective (Peter O'Toole) helps a French art forger's daughter (Audrey Hepburn) steal a fake Venus.
programme.title: How to Steal a Million

programme.desc: Sailor Sinbad (John Phillip Law) fights a one-eyed centaur, a ship's figurehead and other creatures sent by an evil prince (Tom Baker).
programme.title: The Golden Voyage of Sinbad

programme.desc: A cartoonist (Dean Martin) turns his babbling roommate's (Jerry Lewis) bizarre dreams into a hit comic strip read by spies.
programme.title: Artists and Models

programme.desc: A corporate climber (Jack Lemmon), whose boss (Fred MacMurray) and others use his apartment for hanky-panky, aids a young woman (Shirley MacLaine).
programme.title: The Apartment

programme.desc: A heroin addict is caught between two Times Square narcotics agents and the pusher they are trying to bust.
programme.title: Born to Win

programme.desc: The queen of Egypt (Elizabeth Taylor) seduces Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison), but when he is killed, she uses Mark Antony (Richard Burton) as her new protector.
programme.title: Cleopatra

programme.desc: Runyonesque Dave the Dude (Glenn Ford) turns Apple Annie (Bette Davis) into a Manhattan dowager in director Frank Capra's remake of his 1933 Lady for a Day.
programme.title: Pocketful of Miracles

programme.desc: An outlaw is left by his cohorts and girlfriend to be captured after he is wounded during a heist. When he is released from prison, he tracks his old crew to a run-down town and plays a game of cat and mouse with them.
programme.title: Gun the Man Down

programme.desc: Poor sharecroppers, the Joads leave dust bowl Oklahoma in hope of better luck in California.
programme.title: The Grapes of Wrath

programme.desc: A mad professor (Boris Karloff) in New England zaps people in a special cabinet to create an army of supermen.
programme.title: The Boogie Man Will Get You

programme.desc: Sam Craig, a New York sportswriter marries Tess Harding, a political columnist whose career comes first.
programme.title: Woman of the Year

programme.desc: A New York dancer (Fred Astaire) grooms a chorus girl (Judy Garland) to be his new partner, falling in love along the way.
programme.title: Easter Parade

programme.desc: A secret agent (Dean Martin) stops a Chinese villain's atomic plot in the first of four Matt Helm movies.
programme.title: The Silencers

programme.desc: A suave stranger (Cary Grant) helps a chic widow (Audrey Hepburn) stalked by four men looking for loot in Paris.
programme.title: Charade

programme.desc: Mark Twain's boy hero (Eddie Hodges) rafts the Mississippi with slave Jim (Archie Moore) and meets a bogus king (Tony Randall) and duke.
programme.title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn

programme.desc: A ballet dancer (Vivien Leigh) and a British officer (Robert Taylor) cross stars on the eve of World War I.
programme.title: Waterloo Bridge

programme.desc: Herman (Fred Gwynne), Lily (Yvonne De Carlo), Grandpa (Al Lewis) and their ghoulish TV family go to England to claim an estate.
programme.title: Munster, Go Home

programme.desc: Two con artists (James Mason, Gina Lollobrigida) bring an outlaw (Lee Van Cleef) into their scheme to bilk the Mexican government out of $1 million.
programme.title: Bad Man's River

programme.desc: Blondie (Penny Singleton) and Dagwood (Arthur Lake) join Mr. Dithers on a South American cruise.
programme.title: Blondie Goes Latin

programme.desc: A singing cowboy (Roy Rogers) clears the outlaw (Donald Barry) and his gang of a bank robbery done by bankers.
programme.title: Days of Jesse James

programme.desc: The Civil War Map Story Steve goes to Richmond to find out why a foreign agent has stolen an old Civil War map from the home of Col. Caruthers.
programme.title: Dangerous Assignment

programme.desc: S02 E12 — Hot Ice Cream A body goes missing from an amusement park.
programme.title: Man With a Camera

programme.desc: S01 E22 — Cupid's Question Column Blondie is asked to write an advice column for the local newspaper.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: S01 E19 — Lucy's Barbershop Quartet Lucy works to attend a barbershop-quartet competition.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: S02 E01 — Showdown A young bank robber gets a job on the Ponderosa to prevent the sheriff from finding the other members of his gang.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Batman (Adam West) and Robin (Burt Ward) save Gotham City from the Catwoman, Joker (Cesar Romero), Penguin and Riddler.
programme.title: Batman

programme.desc: Three bumbling janitors encounter a slew of curious creatures after accidentally launching a rocket to Venus.
programme.title: Have Rocket, Will Travel

programme.desc: A Broadway star (Bette Davis) takes a young and seemingly naive aspiring actress (Anne Baxter) under her wing.
programme.title: All About Eve

programme.desc: Two Wild West con men (John Astin, Steve Carlson) ride into a Colorado town, unaware it sits on a fortune in ore.
programme.title: The Brothers O'Toole

programme.desc: A bomber pilot (Spencer Tracy) goes to heaven and becomes guardian angel to another pilot (Van Johnson) courting his old girlfriend (Irene Dunne).
programme.title: A Guy Named Joe

programme.desc: A poor Chicago secretary (Diana Ross) takes the fast lane to fame as a model and a fashion designer.
programme.title: Mahogany

programme.desc: Astronauts (John Archer, Warner Anderson, Tom Powers) land their rocket on the moon but must lessen the load a lot in order to get back.
programme.title: Destination Moon

programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Two Navy inductees (Dean Martin, Jerry Lewis) go through basic training, then head for Honolulu by submarine for fun.
programme.title: Sailor Beware

programme.desc: Astronauts (John Archer, Warner Anderson, Tom Powers) land their rocket on the moon but must lessen the load a lot in order to get back.
programme.title: Destination Moon
